name: Fast Auto Loans, Inc.
description_short: Fast Auto Loans, Inc. Tucson, Arizona — Arizona direct lender offering vehicle title loans ($300–$15,000) and registration loans (up to $2,500) with sam...
description_long:
Fast Auto Loans, Inc. is a direct, store-based lender operating exclusively in Arizona, with branches in Glendale, Peoria, and multiple Phoenix locations. The company positions itself as a local emergency cash provider for residents who need funds quickly and cannot or do not want to rely on traditional bank lending. All lending appears to be done in-house — there is no mention of a third-party network or broker arrangement.

The company's two core products are title loans and registration loans. Title loans range from $300 to $15,000 and require the borrower to hand over a lien-free vehicle title as collateral, along with presenting the vehicle for a physical inspection. Registration loans, capped at $2,500, use the borrower's current Arizona vehicle registration rather than a clean title, making them accessible to people still paying off a car loan; they also require proof of income and an active checking account. Both products are marketed as same-day, with the company claiming funds can be received in as little as 30 minutes after completing the in-store process.

What distinguishes Fast Auto Loans from national online title-loan brands is its brick-and-mortar, locally staffed model. Borrowers apply online or by phone, but the transaction closes in person at a physical branch — the Glendale store is at 4752 West Glendale Avenue. The company emphasizes direct local lending, meaning the store representative who calls you back is also the one processing your loan. Spanish-language service is available, and the website is partially bilingual, which is relevant for its Arizona customer base.

Honestly, title loans and registration loans are among the highest-cost consumer credit products available. While Fast Auto Loans does not display APR figures on its location pages — directing users to a separate Rates, Fees & Terms page — Arizona-licensed title lenders typically charge triple-digit APRs. The risk is significant: borrowers who cannot repay may lose their vehicle. Registration loans are slightly less risky in that the lender does not hold the title, but they still carry high rates and are tied to the vehicle. This product is appropriate only as a true last resort for borrowers who have exhausted lower-cost options.
pros: Same-day cash up to $15,000 on title loans — one of the higher loan ceilings among storefront title lenders
Registration loans available up to $2,500 — no lien-free title required, accessible to borrowers still financing their vehicle
All credit types accepted — approval based primarily on vehicle condition and documents, not credit score
Online application available with in-store close — borrowers can start the process remotely and finish quickly
Multiple Arizona locations (Glendale, Peoria, two Phoenix stores) for in-person convenience
30-minute estimated turnaround from approval to cash disbursement
Bilingual service (English and Spanish) with a partially translated website
cons: Arizona-only lender — no service available outside the state
Title loans require a lien-free vehicle title as collateral — defaulting risks vehicle repossession
APR and fee disclosures are not shown on location pages — borrowers must navigate to a separate Rates, Fees & Terms page before knowing the cost
Registration loans capped at $2,500, limiting usefulness for larger emergencies
In-person store visit required to close the loan — fully remote funding is not available
best_for: Arizona residents with a paid-off vehicle who need $300–$15,000 in same-day emergency cash
Borrowers with poor or no credit who cannot qualify for a personal loan or credit card
People still financing their vehicle who need up to $2,500 quickly (registration loan path)
Spanish-speaking borrowers in the Glendale, Peoria, or Phoenix metro who prefer local bilingual service
diagnosis: Fast Auto Loans, Inc. is best suited for Arizona residents facing a genuine financial emergency who own their vehicle outright and have exhausted lower-cost borrowing options. The main caveat is cost: title and registration loans carry high interest rates typical of the industry, and borrowers who cannot repay risk losing their vehicle, making this a product to use with caution and only when repayment is realistic.
services: Online title loans (application starts digitally, closes in store)
Phone consultation and callback from local branch representative
Multiple Arizona branch locations (Glendale, Peoria, Phoenix)
Online inquiry form with direct routing to local store
